title: Packed Syrian Blackeye Pea Stew
description: An earthy, dense Syrian blackeye pea stew that feels like a hearty brunch, finished with a glossy dairy‑free tahini glaze.
instructions: Rinse the blackeye peas and soak them for at least 8 hours before cooking.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat, then add the diced root vegetables and sauté until they soften and lightly caramelize.
Stir in the minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper, cooking for about a minute until fragrant.
Drain the soaked peas and add them to the pot, then pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes or until the peas are tender and the stew thickens.
While the stew simmers, whisk together the tahini, lemon juice, and maple syrup until smooth to create a lacquer glaze.
Remove the bay leaf, taste the stew, adjust seasoning if needed, then stir in the glaze until the stew takes on a glossy sheen.
Serve the stew hot, optionally garnished with chopped parsley, for a comforting, dairy‑free brunch meal.
